name: Great Antipasto
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Artichoke hearts; jar of marinated
1 cup Tomato sauce; or 8 oz can
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, combine the carrots, peppers, cauliflower, mushrooms, pickles, celery, black olives, onions, artichoke hearts, tomato sauce (or canned tomatoes), ketchup, and olive oil.
2. Bring to a boil over high heat.
3. Reduce heat and simmer, covered, for 20 to 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the carrots are tender crisp.
4. Drain any excess liquid from tuna, if using, and add it to the vegetable mixture.
5. Simmer for an additional 5 minutes, allowing the tuna to break up into smaller pieces.
6. Transfer the mixture to serving containers.
7. Store in the refrigerator for up to one week or freeze for up to three months.
8. Best made with locally grown vegetables in late summer.
9. Serve on toasted French bread or as a spread on crackers.
